<content>
1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
3: 
4: package census
5: 
6: import (
7: 	"context"
8: 	"time"
9: 
10: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/db"
11: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/errors"
12: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/scheduler"
13: )
14: 
15: var (
16: 	NewCensusJobFn = newCensusJob
17: 	RunFn          = runInternal
18: )
19: 
20: type censusJob struct {
21: 	r                db.Reader
22: 	w                db.Writer
23: 	lurEnabled       bool
24: 	emitEvents       bool
25: 	sessionsAgent    any
26: 	activeUsersAgent any
27: 	eventCtx         context.Context
28: }

31: 	const op = "censusJob.newCensusJob"

37: 	}

48: }

51: func (c *censusJob) Status() scheduler.JobStatus {

53: }
54: 
55: // Run performs the required work depending on the implementation.
56: // The context is used to notify the job that it should exit early.

58: 	err := RunFn(ctx, c)
59: 	return err

63: 	return nil
64: }

67: // Census will run every hour to ensure any interrupted jobs will be re-attempted
68: func (c *censusJob) NextRunIn(_ context.Context) (time.Duration, error) {
69: 	return time.Hour, nil
70: }

4: package job
5: 
6: import (
7: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/scheduler/job/store"
8: 	"google.golang.org/protobuf/proto"
9: )
10: 
11: // Job represents work that should be run at a predetermined time and needs to be synchronized
12: // across servers to ensure that one and only one instance of a job is running at any given time.
13: type Job struct {
14: 	*store.Job
15: 	tableName string `gorm:"-"`
16: }
17: 
18: func (j *Job) clone() *Job {
19: 	cp := proto.Clone(j.Job)
20: 	return &Job{
21: 		Job:       cp.(*store.Job),
22: 		tableName: j.tableName,
23: 	}
24: }
25: 
26: func allocJob() *Job {
27: 	return &Job{
28: 		Job: &store.Job{},
29: 	}
30: }
31: 
32: // TableName returns the table name for the job.
33: func (j *Job) TableName() string {
34: 	if j.tableName != "" {
35: 		return j.tableName
36: 	}
37: 	return "job"
38: }
39: 
40: // SetTableName sets the table name. If the caller attempts to
41: // set the name to "" the name will be reset to the default name "job".
42: func (j *Job) SetTableName(n string) {
43: 	j.tableName = n
44: }

200: 				"server id", s.serverId)
201: 			wg.Wait()

212: 
213: func (s *Scheduler) schedule(ctx context.Context, wg *sync.WaitGroup) {
214: 	const op = "scheduler.(Scheduler).schedule"
215: 	repo, err := s.jobRepoFn()
216: 	if err != nil {
217: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error creating job repo"))
218: 		return
219: 	}
220: 
221: 	runs, err := repo.RunJobs(ctx, s.serverId, job.WithRunJobsLimit(s.runJobsLimit))
222: 	if err != nil {
223: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error getting jobs to run from repo"))
224: 		return
225: 	}
226: 
227: 	for _, r := range runs {
228: 		err := s.runJob(ctx, wg, r)
229: 		if err != nil {
230: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error starting job"))
231: 			if _, inner := repo.FailRun(ctx, r.PrivateId, 0, 0); inner != nil {
232: 				event.WriteError(ctx, op, inner, event.WithInfoMsg("error updating failed job run"))
233: 			}
234: 		}
235: 	}
236: }
237: 
238: func (s *Scheduler) runJob(ctx context.Context, wg *sync.WaitGroup, r *job.Run) error {
239: 	const op = "scheduler.(Scheduler).runJob"
240: 	regJob, ok := s.registeredJobs.Load(r.JobName)
241: 	if !ok {
242: 		return fmt.Errorf("job %q not registered on scheduler", r.JobName)
243: 	}
244: 
245: 	repo, err := s.jobRepoFn()
246: 	if err != nil {
247: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create job repository: %w", err)
248: 	}
249: 
250: 	j := regJob.(Job)
251: 	rj := &runningJob{runId: r.PrivateId, status: j.Status}
252: 	_, loaded := s.runningJobs.LoadOrStore(r.JobName, rj)
253: 	if loaded {
254: 		return fmt.Errorf("job %q is already running", r.JobName)
255: 	}
256: 	var jobContext context.Context
257: 	jobContext, rj.cancelCtx = context.WithCancel(ctx)
258: 
259: 	wg.Add(1)
260: 	go func() {
261: 		defer rj.cancelCtx()
262: 		defer wg.Done()
263: 		runErr := j.Run(jobContext)
264: 
265: 		// Get final status report to update run progress with
266: 		status := j.Status()
267: 		var updateErr error
268: 		switch {
269: 		case ctx.Err() != nil:
270: 			// Base context is no longer valid, skip repo updates as they will fail and exit
271: 		case runErr == nil:
272: 			nextRun, inner := j.NextRunIn(ctx)
273: 			if inner != nil {
274: 				event.WriteError(ctx, op, inner, event.WithInfoMsg("error getting next run time", "name", j.Name()))
275: 			}
276: 			_, updateErr = repo.CompleteRun(ctx, r.PrivateId, nextRun, status.Completed, status.Total)
277: 		default:
278: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, runErr, event.WithInfoMsg("job run failed", "run id", r.PrivateId, "name", j.Name()))
279: 			_, updateErr = repo.FailRun(ctx, r.PrivateId, status.Completed, status.Total)
280: 		}
281: 
282: 		if updateErr != nil {
283: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, updateErr, event.WithInfoMsg("error updating job run", "name", j.Name()))
284: 		}
285: 		s.runningJobs.Delete(j.Name())
286: 	}()
287: 
288: 	return nil
289: }
290: 
291: func (s *Scheduler) monitorJobs(ctx context.Context) {
292: 	const op = "scheduler.(Scheduler).monitorJobs"
293: 	event.WriteSysEvent(ctx, op, "monitor loop running",
294: 		"server id", s.serverId,
295: 		"monitor interval", s.monitorInterval.String(),
296: 		"interrupt threshold", s.interruptThreshold.String())
297: 	timer := time.NewTimer(0)
298: 	for {
299: 		select {
300: 		case <-ctx.Done():
301: 			event.WriteSysEvent(ctx, op, "job monitor loop shutting down")
302: 			return
303: 
304: 		case <-timer.C:
305: 			// Update progress of all running jobs
306: 			s.runningJobs.Range(func(_, v any) bool {
307: 				err := s.updateRunningJobProgress(ctx, v.(*runningJob))
308: 				if err != nil {
309: 					event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error updating job progress"))
310: 				}
311: 				return true
312: 			})
313: 
314: 			// Check for defunct runs to interrupt
315: 			repo, err := s.jobRepoFn()
316: 			if err != nil {
317: 				event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error creating job repo"))
318: 				break
319: 			}
320: 
321: 			_, err = repo.InterruptRuns(ctx, s.interruptThreshold)
322: 			if err != nil {
323: 				event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error interrupting job runs"))
324: 			}
325: 		}
326: 		timer.Reset(s.monitorInterval)
327: 	}
328: }
329: 
330: func (s *Scheduler) updateRunningJobProgress(ctx context.Context, j *runningJob) error {
331: 	repo, err := s.jobRepoFn()
332: 	if err != nil {
333: 		return fmt.Errorf("error creating job repo %w", err)
334: 	}
335: 	status := j.status()
336: 	_, err = repo.UpdateProgress(ctx, j.runId, status.Completed, status.Total)
337: 	if errors.Match(errors.T(errors.InvalidJobRunState), err) {
338: 		// Job has been persisted with a final run status, cancel job context to trigger early exit.
339: 		j.cancelCtx()
340: 		return nil
341: 	}
342: 	if err != nil {
343: 		return err
344: 	}
345: 
346: 	return nil
347: }
348: 
349: // GetRunJobsInterval returns the value runJobsInterval,
350: // which represents an interval at which the scheduler
351: // will query the repository for jobs to run.
352: func (s *Scheduler) GetRunJobsInterval() time.Duration {
353: 	return s.runJobsInterval
354: }

(End of file - total 354 lines)
</content>

1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
3: 
4: package servers
5: 
6: import (
7: 	"context"
8: 	"time"
9: 
10: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/db"
11: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/errors"
12: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/kms"
13: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/scheduler"
14: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/server"
15: 	"github.com/hashicorp/nodeenrollment"
16: )
17: 
18: // rotateRootsJob defines a periodic job that initiates root certificate rotation
19: // It runs every hour; the root rotation function in the library is designed to not
20: // do anything if it's not time to rotate (roots are within their valid ranges)
21: type rotateRootsJob struct {
22: 	workerAuthRepo *server.WorkerAuthRepositoryStorage
23: 
24: 	totalRotates int
25: 
26: 	rotateFrequency time.Duration
27: 
28: 	certificateLifetime time.Duration
29: }
30: 
31: // newRotateRootsJob instantiates the rotate roots job.
32: func newRotateRootsJob(ctx context.Context, r db.Reader, w db.Writer, kms *kms.Kms, opt ...Option) (*rotateRootsJob, error) {
33: 	const op = "server.newRotateRootsJob"
34: 	switch {
35: 	case isNil(r):
36: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing reader")
37: 	case isNil(w):
38: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing writer")
39: 	case kms == nil:
40: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing kms")
41: 	}
42: 
43: 	workerAuthRepo, err := server.NewRepositoryStorage(ctx, r, w, kms)
44: 	if err != nil {
45: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
46: 	}
47: 
48: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
49: 
50: 	return &rotateRootsJob{
51: 		workerAuthRepo:      workerAuthRepo,
52: 		totalRotates:        0,
53: 		rotateFrequency:     opts.withRotationFrequency,
54: 		certificateLifetime: opts.withCertificateLifetime,
55: 	}, nil
56: }
57: 
58: // Name returns a short, unique name for the job.
59: func (r *rotateRootsJob) Name() string { return "rotate_roots" }
60: 
61: // Description returns the description for the job.
62: func (r *rotateRootsJob) Description() string {
63: 	return "Rotate root certificates"
64: }
65: 
66: // NextRunIn returns the next run time after a job is completed.
67: // This is represented by RotateFrequency
68: func (r *rotateRootsJob) NextRunIn(_ context.Context) (time.Duration, error) {
69: 	return r.rotateFrequency, nil
70: }
71: 
72: // Status returns the status of the running job.
73: func (r *rotateRootsJob) Status() scheduler.JobStatus {
74: 	return scheduler.JobStatus{
75: 		Completed: r.totalRotates,
76: 		Total:     r.totalRotates,
77: 	}
78: }
79: 
80: // Run executes the job by calling the rotateRoots domain function
81: func (r *rotateRootsJob) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
82: 	const op = "server.(rotateRootsJob).Run"
83: 
84: 	_, err := server.RotateRoots(ctx, r.workerAuthRepo, nodeenrollment.WithCertificateLifetime(r.certificateLifetime))
85: 	if err != nil {
86: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
87: 	}
88: 
89: 	r.totalRotates += 1
90: 
91: 	return nil
92: }

(End of file - total 92 lines)
